definitions.text: Bright; luminous; not dark or obscured.
examples: The windshield was clear and clean.
Congress passed the President’s Clear Skies legislation.
The driver had mistakenly thought the intersection was clear.
I threw it clear across the river to the other side.

definitions.text: Not near something or touching it.
examples: Stand clear of the rails, a train is coming.

definitions.text: To remove obstructions, impediments or other unwanted items from.
examples: Police took two hours to clear the road.
If you clear the table, I'll wash up.

definitions.text: To remove (items or material) so as to leave something unobstructed or open.
examples: Please clear all this stuff off the table.
The loggers came and cleared the trees.

definitions.text: To leave abruptly; to clear off or clear out.

examples: The deer were standing in the clear.

definitions.text: Full extent; distance between extreme limits; especially; the distance between the nearest surfaces of two bodies, or the space between walls.
examples: a room ten feet square in the clear

definitions.text: The completion of a stage or challenge, or of the whole game.
examples: It took me weeks to achieve a one-credit clear (1CC).
